forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KS36KNHH9BPC82MFMGTY3T5J
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 9c80
+project: command-service
+title: MCP notification surface for the event system (bus â†’ serverâ†’client notifications)
+---
+## What
+
+Give the event system an MCP face so any client â€” the kanban webview AND AI agents â€” subscribes to the same change stream over MCP. Today change events live on an in-process `tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<EntityEvent>` (`swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs:175`) plus store `ChangeEvent`s (`swissarmyhammer-store/src/event.rs`), and reach the UI only via Tauri `app.emit("entity-field-changed", â€¦)` / `app.emit("board-changed", â€¦)` etc. There is NO MCP notification surface (only code-context's `notifications/progress`). Build one.
+
+### Design
+
+The in-process broadcast channel stays the **bus**. Add a **notification bridge** that subscribes to it and fans events out as MCP serverâ†’client `notifications/â€¦` over every connected transport. Clients subscribe; the bridge delivers.
+
+- **Event vocabulary on the wire** â€” keep events thin (the event-architecture rule): entity-level create/remove + field-level `{field, value}`; no enrichment, no re-fetch. Normalize today's two shapes (`EntityEvent` enum + store `ChangeEvent` name/payload) into one notification schema. Candidate names: `notifications/entity/changed`, `notifications/entity/deleted`, `notifications/attachment/changed`, `notifications/store/undo_changed` (the stack-state event from the change-propagation task).
+- **Transport** â€” the plugin platform's `McpServer`/transport layer must carry serverâ†’client notifications: in-process (for the webview's in-process MCP client and host UI code), and out to CLI/URL clients (AI agents) over their MCP connections. rmcp supports notifications; wire delivery + a subscription registry (which client wants which streams).
+- **Producers** â€” `kanban`, `views`, `ui_state`, `store` servers emit through the bridge rather than calling `app.emit`. They already mutate the broadcast bus (entity cache) â€” the bridge picks those up; servers without a cache (e.g. `store` stack-state) emit explicitly.
+
+Files:
+- New notification-bridge module in the host/plugin layer â€” subscribes to `broadcast::Receiver<EntityEvent>` (+ store `flush_changes`) and emits MCP notifications
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/...` (transport/dispatch) â€” serverâ†’client notification delivery + per-client subscription registry
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` â€” wire the bridge at bootstrap
+
+This task is the generic surface. The undo change-propagation task defines *what* undo/redo emit into it; the frontend task migrates the webview onto it.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A change made via any server (entity edit, perspective change) produces an MCP `notifications/â€¦changed` deliverable to a subscribed MCP client
+- [ ] An in-process MCP client and an external (stdio/URL) MCP client both receive the same notification stream
+- [ ] Notification payloads are thin (entity/field level), one normalized schema for entity + store events
+- [ ] The bridge sources from the existing broadcast bus â€” no duplicate event production
+- [ ] `notifications/store/undo_changed` (stack-state) is deliverable for the undo controls
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/mcp_notifications_e2e.rs` â€” boot host with `kanban`; subscribe an in-process MCP client; edit a task; assert the client receives `notifications/entity/changed` with the thin `{field,value}` payload; delete â†’ `notifications/entity/deleted`
+- [ ] An external-transport client (CliServer fixture) subscribes and receives the same notification â€” proves AI agents get the stream
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service --test integration mcp_notifications_e2e` passes
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write the in-process subscribeâ†’editâ†’receive test first.
+
+Prerequisite for the change-propagation task and the frontend MCP-notification migration. Coordinates with `single-changelog` (one event schema). Depends on the plugin-platform transport layer.
\ No newline at end of file

Candidate names: `notifications/entity/changed`, `notifications/entity/deleted`, `notifications/attachment/changed`, `notifications/store/undo_changed` (the stack-state event from the change-propagation task).
-- **Transport** â€” the plugin platform's `McpServer`/transport layer must carry serverâ†’client notifications: in-process (for the webview's in-process MCP client and host UI code), and out to CLI/URL clients (AI agents) over their MCP connections. rmcp supports notifications; wire delivery + a subscription registry (which client wants which streams).

-

-
-## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] An external-transport client (CliServer fixture) subscribes and receives the same notification â€” proves AI agents get the stream

-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` â€” write the in-process subscribeâ†’editâ†’receive test first.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,40 +6,68 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: 9c80
 project: command-service
-title: MCP notification surface for the event system (bus â†’ serverâ†’client notifications)
+title: 'MCP notification surface: store-keyed change events for all stored things (not just entities)'
 ---
 ## What

-Give the event system an MCP face so any client â€” the kanban webview AND AI agents â€” subscribes to the same change stream over MCP. Today change events live on an in-process `tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<EntityEvent>` (`swissarmyhammer-entity/src/cache.rs:175`) plus store `ChangeEvent`s (`swissarmyhammer-store/src/event.rs`), and reach the UI only via Tauri `app.emit("entity-field-changed", â€¦)` / `app.emit("board-changed", â€¦)` etc. There is NO MCP notification surface (only code-context's `notifications/progress`). Build one.
+Give the event system an MCP face so any client â€” the kanban webview AND AI agents â€” subscribes to the same change stream over MCP. Crucially, the schema must be **generic over "stored things," not entity-specific.** There are three `TrackedStore`-backed categories (verified), and "entity" is only one:
+
+- **Entities** â€” task/tag/column/project/actor (`EntityTypeStore`). Emit rich **field-level** `EntityEvent`s via `broadcast::Sender<EntityEvent>` (`cache.rs:175`).
+- **Views** â€” `ViewStore` (`views/store.rs`). Store-tracked + undoable, NOT an entity; no field-level diff today.
+- **Perspectives** â€” `PerspectiveStore` (`swissarmyhammer-perspectives` crate). Same.
+
+Plus stored-but-not-tracked state: **UIState** (own JSON, not undoable) and `undo_stack.yaml`.
+
+Today only entities emit thin events; views/perspectives surface as coarse store `ChangeEvent`s (`store/event.rs`, names like `"perspective-changed"`) or Tauri `app.emit`. There is no MCP notification surface (only code-context `notifications/progress`). Build a generic one.
+
+### Schema â€” store-keyed, not entity-keyed
+
+One notification family for all store-backed stored things:
+
+```
+notifications/store/changed {
+  store: string,        // "task" | "tag" | "view" | "perspective" | ...
+  item:  string,        // item id
+  op:    "created" | "removed" | "updated",
+  changes?: [{ field, value }]   // present for stores that produce field diffs (entities);
+                                 // absent for coarse stores (views/perspectives) â†’ reload the item
+}
+```
+
+- **Entity stores** populate `changes` (field-level, `value: null` = removed) â€” the existing thin `EntityEvent` projected into the generic shape.
+- **Views/perspectives** omit `changes` â†’ consumers reload the named item. (When `single-changelog` unifies the per-store diff format, they can carry field-level `changes` too â€” no schema change needed.)
+- **Stack state**: `notifications/store/undo_changed { can_undo, can_redo, undo_label?, redo_label? }` (from the change-propagation task).
+
+Separate, non-undoable family for ephemeral UI state (not a stored thing in the substrate):
+```
+notifications/ui_state/changed { window?, key, value }   // palette/inspector/mode/focus/drag
+```

-### Design
+### Mechanism

-The in-process broadcast channel stays the **bus**. Add a **notification bridge** that subscribes to it and fans events out as MCP serverâ†’client `notifications/â€¦` over every connected transport. Clients subscribe; the bridge delivers.
+In-process broadcast channel stays the **bus**. A **notification bridge** subscribes to it (entity `EntityEvent` + store `ChangeEvent` from `flush_changes` + `ui_state` changes) and fans out as MCP serverâ†’client `notifications/â€¦` over every transport. Clients subscribe; the bridge delivers.

-- **Event vocabulary on the wire** â€” keep events thin (the event-architecture rule): entity-level create/remove + field-level `{field, value}`; no enrichment, no re-fetch. Normalize today's two shapes (`EntityEvent` enum + store `ChangeEvent` name/payload) into one notification schema. Candidate names: `notifications/entity/changed`, `notifications/entity/deleted`, `notifications/attachment/changed`, `notifications/store/undo_changed` (the stack-state event from the change-propagation task).
-- **Transport** â€” the plugin platform's `McpServer`/transport layer must carry serverâ†’client notifications: in-process (for the webview's in-process MCP client and host UI code), and out to CLI/URL clients (AI agents) over their MCP connections. rmcp supports notifications; wire delivery + a subscription registry (which client wants which streams).
-- **Producers** â€” `kanban`, `views`, `ui_state`, `store` servers emit through the bridge rather than calling `app.emit`. They already mutate the broadcast bus (entity cache) â€” the bridge picks those up; servers without a cache (e.g. `store` stack-state) emit explicitly.
+- **Transport** â€” the plugin platform's `McpServer`/transport layer carries serverâ†’client notifications: in-process (webview's MCP client, host UI) and out to CLI/URL clients (agents). rmcp supports notifications; wire delivery + a per-client subscription registry.
+- **Producers** â€” `kanban`/`views`/`store`/`ui_state` servers emit through the bridge instead of `app.emit`. Entity stores already feed the broadcast bus; views/perspectives feed coarse events; `ui_state` feeds the separate UI family.

 Files:
-- New notification-bridge module in the host/plugin layer â€” subscribes to `broadcast::Receiver<EntityEvent>` (+ store `flush_changes`) and emits MCP notifications
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/...` (transport/dispatch) â€” serverâ†’client notification delivery + per-client subscription registry
+- New notification-bridge module (host/plugin layer) â€” subscribes to the bus(es), normalizes to the store-keyed schema, emits MCP notifications
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/...` (transport/dispatch) â€” serverâ†’client notification delivery + subscription registry
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` â€” wire the bridge at bootstrap

-This task is the generic surface. The undo change-propagation task defines *what* undo/redo emit into it; the frontend task migrates the webview onto it.
-
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A change made via any server (entity edit, perspective change) produces an MCP `notifications/â€¦changed` deliverable to a subscribed MCP client
-- [ ] An in-process MCP client and an external (stdio/URL) MCP client both receive the same notification stream
-- [ ] Notification payloads are thin (entity/field level), one normalized schema for entity + store events
-- [ ] The bridge sources from the existing broadcast bus â€” no duplicate event production
-- [ ] `notifications/store/undo_changed` (stack-state) is deliverable for the undo controls
+- [ ] One generic `notifications/store/changed { store, item, op, changes? }` schema covers entities AND views AND perspectives
+- [ ] Entity changes carry field-level `changes`; view/perspective changes omit `changes` (reload-item) and still notify with the right `store`/`item`/`op`
+- [ ] `notifications/store/undo_changed` (stack state) and `notifications/ui_state/changed` (ephemeral UI) are distinct families
+- [ ] In-process and external (stdio/URL) MCP clients both receive the stream â€” proves agents get it
+- [ ] The bridge sources from the existing buses â€” no duplicate event production

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/mcp_notifications_e2e.rs` â€” boot host with `kanban`; subscribe an in-process MCP client; edit a task; assert the client receives `notifications/entity/changed` with the thin `{field,value}` payload; delete â†’ `notifications/entity/deleted`
-- [ ] An external-transport client (CliServer fixture) subscribes and receives the same notification â€” proves AI agents get the stream
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/mcp_notifications_e2e.rs` â€” subscribe an in-process MCP client; edit a task â†’ `store/changed { store:"task", op:"updated", changes:[â€¦] }`; change a perspective filter â†’ `store/changed { store:"perspective", op:"updated" }` (no `changes`); toggle palette â†’ `ui_state/changed`
+- [ ] External-transport client (CliServer fixture) receives the same `store/changed` stream
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service --test integration mcp_notifications_e2e` passes

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` â€” write the in-process subscribeâ†’editâ†’receive test first.
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write the store-keyed subscribeâ†’editâ†’receive test first, covering an entity AND a perspective so the generic schema is exercised from the start.

-Prerequisite for the change-propagation task and the frontend MCP-notification migration. Coordinates with `single-changelog` (one event schema). Depends on the plugin-platform transport layer.
\ No newline at end of file
+Prerequisite for the change-propagation and frontend-migration tasks. Coordinates with `single-changelog` (uniform per-store diffs let views/perspectives carry field-level changes). Depends on the plugin-platform transport layer.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@
 - claude-code
 depends_on:
 - 01KS36KNHH9BPC82MFMGTY3T5J
+- 01KS5F7BR6850RKT67X4CNHPAZ
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: 9c80
 project: command-service
@@ -10,64 +11,71 @@
 ---
 ## What

-Give the event system an MCP face so any client â€” the kanban webview AND AI agents â€” subscribes to the same change stream over MCP. Crucially, the schema must be **generic over "stored things," not entity-specific.** There are three `TrackedStore`-backed categories (verified), and "entity" is only one:
+Give the event system an MCP face so any client â€” the kanban webview AND AI agents â€” subscribes to the same change stream over MCP. The model has **four planes**, all woven together by **transaction correlation (`txn`) + provenance (`origin`)** on every notification. The schema is **generic over "stored things," not entity-specific** â€” there are three `TrackedStore` categories (verified) and "entity" is only one:

-- **Entities** â€” task/tag/column/project/actor (`EntityTypeStore`). Emit rich **field-level** `EntityEvent`s via `broadcast::Sender<EntityEvent>` (`cache.rs:175`).
-- **Views** â€” `ViewStore` (`views/store.rs`). Store-tracked + undoable, NOT an entity; no field-level diff today.
-- **Perspectives** â€” `PerspectiveStore` (`swissarmyhammer-perspectives` crate). Same.
+- **Entities** â€” task/tag/column/project/actor (`EntityTypeStore`). Rich **field-level** `EntityEvent`s via `broadcast::Sender<EntityEvent>` (`cache.rs:175`).
+- **Views** â€” `ViewStore` (`views/store.rs`). Tracked + undoable, NOT an entity; no field-level diff today.
+- **Perspectives** â€” `PerspectiveStore` (`swissarmyhammer-perspectives`). Same.
+- Plus stored-but-not-tracked: **UIState** (own JSON, not undoable) and `undo_stack.yaml`.

-Plus stored-but-not-tracked state: **UIState** (own JSON, not undoable) and `undo_stack.yaml`.
-
-Today only entities emit thin events; views/perspectives surface as coarse store `ChangeEvent`s (`store/event.rs`, names like `"perspective-changed"`) or Tauri `app.emit`. There is no MCP notification surface (only code-context `notifications/progress`). Build a generic one.
+## The four notification planes

-### Schema â€” store-keyed, not entity-keyed
-
-One notification family for all store-backed stored things:
-
+### 1. Data changes (store-keyed) â€” what changed
 ```
 notifications/store/changed {
-  store: string,        // "task" | "tag" | "view" | "perspective" | ...
-  item:  string,        // item id
-  op:    "created" | "removed" | "updated",
-  changes?: [{ field, value }]   // present for stores that produce field diffs (entities);
-                                 // absent for coarse stores (views/perspectives) â†’ reload the item
+  store, item, op: "created"|"removed"|"updated",
+  changes?: [{ field, value }],   // entities carry field diffs; views/perspectives omit â†’ reload item
+  txn,                            // correlation: the transaction/undo-group this change belongs to
+  origin                          // provenance: "user" | "agent:<id>" | "undo" | "redo" | "watcher"
 }
 ```
+Entity stores populate `changes` (value:null = removed); views/perspectives omit it (reload-item) until `single-changelog` unifies diff formats.
+
+### 2. Action/command events (semantic) â€” what was done
+```
+notifications/commands/executed { id, ctx, result, txn, origin }
+```
+Emitted by the Command service after a successful `execute` (see the execute-verb task). Lets reactive (Obsidian-style) plugins subscribe to intent â€” "on task created", "on perspective switched" â€” not raw diffs. Shares the `txn` with the data changes the command produced, so a consumer can correlate "this action â†’ these data changes."

-- **Entity stores** populate `changes` (field-level, `value: null` = removed) â€” the existing thin `EntityEvent` projected into the generic shape.
-- **Views/perspectives** omit `changes` â†’ consumers reload the named item. (When `single-changelog` unifies the per-store diff format, they can carry field-level `changes` too â€” no schema change needed.)
-- **Stack state**: `notifications/store/undo_changed { can_undo, can_redo, undo_label?, redo_label? }` (from the change-propagation task).
+### 3. Registry / lifecycle
+- `notifications/commands/changed` â€” command registry changed (palette refresh); already in command-service.md.
+- `notifications/tools/list_changed`, server-registry, plugin load/unload, board opened/closed/switched â€” platform/lifecycle.

-Separate, non-undoable family for ephemeral UI state (not a stored thing in the substrate):
+### 4. Ephemeral UI state (not a stored thing, not undoable)
 ```
 notifications/ui_state/changed { window?, key, value }   // palette/inspector/mode/focus/drag
 ```
+Plus `notifications/store/undo_changed { can_undo, can_redo, undo_label?, redo_label? }` (stack state, from the change-propagation task).
+
+## Correlation + provenance (cross-cutting)

-### Mechanism
+- **`txn`** is the ambient transaction id from the `store` server task â€” the same id that groups undo entries. The Command service generates one per `execute`, propagates it via `RequestContext::extensions`; store writes stamp both the undo `group_id` AND the emitted change events with it. Consumers coalesce by `txn` â†’ one atomic UI update per command; undo emits the inverse batch under a new `txn`.
+- **`origin`** is derived from `CallerId` (already threaded) plus an undo/redo/watcher marker. Enables attribution and echo-suppression for multi-client/agent scenarios.

-In-process broadcast channel stays the **bus**. A **notification bridge** subscribes to it (entity `EntityEvent` + store `ChangeEvent` from `flush_changes` + `ui_state` changes) and fans out as MCP serverâ†’client `notifications/â€¦` over every transport. Clients subscribe; the bridge delivers.
+## Mechanism

-- **Transport** â€” the plugin platform's `McpServer`/transport layer carries serverâ†’client notifications: in-process (webview's MCP client, host UI) and out to CLI/URL clients (agents). rmcp supports notifications; wire delivery + a per-client subscription registry.
-- **Producers** â€” `kanban`/`views`/`store`/`ui_state` servers emit through the bridge instead of `app.emit`. Entity stores already feed the broadcast bus; views/perspectives feed coarse events; `ui_state` feeds the separate UI family.
+In-process broadcast channel stays the **bus**. A **notification bridge** subscribes (entity `EntityEvent` + store `ChangeEvent` + ui_state + command-service action events), normalizes to the schemas above with `txn`/`origin`, and fans out as MCP serverâ†’client `notifications/â€¦` over every transport (in-process for the webview/host; stdio/URL for agents). Per-client subscription registry.

 Files:
-- New notification-bridge module (host/plugin layer) â€” subscribes to the bus(es), normalizes to the store-keyed schema, emits MCP notifications
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/...` (transport/dispatch) â€” serverâ†’client notification delivery + subscription registry
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` â€” wire the bridge at bootstrap
+- New notification-bridge module (host/plugin layer)
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/...` â€” serverâ†’client notification delivery + subscription registry; thread `txn`/`origin` through dispatch
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` â€” wire at bootstrap

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] One generic `notifications/store/changed { store, item, op, changes? }` schema covers entities AND views AND perspectives
-- [ ] Entity changes carry field-level `changes`; view/perspective changes omit `changes` (reload-item) and still notify with the right `store`/`item`/`op`

-- [ ] In-process and external (stdio/URL) MCP clients both receive the stream â€” proves agents get it
-- [ ] The bridge sources from the existing buses â€” no duplicate event production

+- [ ] Bridge sources from existing buses â€” no duplicate event production

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] One generic `store/changed { store, item, op, changes?, txn, origin }` covers entities AND views AND perspectives
-- [ ] The bridge **delivers** the engine-emitted `commands/executed { id, ctx, result, txn, origin }` to subscribed clients (delivery + normalization only; emission timing is owned by `01KS613VPH2G4ZWKZPGW9ZCJAA`)
+- [ ] `commands/executed { id, ctx, result, txn, origin }` fires after each successful command execute
 - [ ] Every notification carries `txn` and `origin`; a command's N data changes share the command's `txn`; undo emits the inverse set under a new `txn` with `origin:"undo"`
 - [ ] `commands/changed`, `ui_state/changed`, `store/undo_changed` are distinct families
 - [ ] In-process AND external (stdio/URL) MCP clients receive the stream
